text: The surgeon evaluates the anatomy and identifies the base of the appendix, tracing it to the tail. The appendix, affected by retrocecal appendicitis, is freed from surrounding structures.

The surgeon elevates the freed tail of the appendix into the surgical field and creates a tunnel between the appendix and mesoappendix. The mesoappendix and appendiceal artery are coagulated using a bipolar energy source from fenestrated bipolar dissectors, followed by transection with a monopolar energy source from curved scissors. This process is repeated stepwise until the appendix is completely freed from the mesoappendix and the blood supply is controlled.

The surgeon ties three simple interrupted sutures around the base of the elevated appendix. Using electrocautery and sharp dissection, the base of the appendix is transected, leaving the appendiceal stump. A purse string suture is then created around the appendiceal stump to invert it within the cecum.
